historyId: h_v3_ACCUMULATION_L40_Saving money for a specific goal

goal: save money for a used car
target total cost: $15,000 (including taxes and repair buffer)
constraints: preference to keep funds in high-yield savings account (HYSA) rather than stock market
I won't touch my emergency cushion to fund this purchase.
decisions: Prioritize cancelling small recurring subscriptions first as a quick win.
Automate savings transfers to a separate labeled account to build the goal faster.
Windfalls: set aside estimated taxes if freelance income; shore up emergency fund if needed; apply remaining to the goal; allocate a small portion for a reward.
Set car fund target to $15,000 (including taxes and repair buffer)
Move car fund to a high-yield savings account (HYSA) instead of stock market investments.

Evaluate how to deploy an extra $200/month: (a) add to the car fund by increasing automated transfers to $867/month to finish sooner; (b) allocate to the car fund by reducing automated transfer to $467/month (total $667) to ease monthly burden.
Automate savings transfers to a labeled HYSA sub-account.
Year-end December bonus: allocate to car HYSA and adjust monthly contributions accordingly.
open: What’s your target deadline or timeline?
Any constraints I should know about (debt, fixed expenses, risk tolerance)?
What’s your current emergency fund balance?
